low_confidence_entries.text: The ODL will deliver high-quality library electronic content to all Ontario residents in order to
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: assist people as they learn, work, and enhance their quality of life. The business plan to be

low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: implementation and operational stages
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: First, some background on the ODL. For the last two years the Ontario Library Association
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: has acted as a catalyst, bringing together the stakeholders needed to create the Ontario
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Digital Library (ODL). These stakeholders include Ontario’s 5000+ public libraries, university,
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Libraries that serve very different clientele recognize that by working together they can create
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: seamless access to quality electronic library services. Through the ODL, students will be
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: able to explore a topic at school, use the same child-safe resources after school (either at
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: home or at a public library), enter college or university with a knowledge of how to use
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: electronic resources that are still available and then, after graduation, continue to use these
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We are moving into a period of convergence. With convergence we no longer have to license
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: commercial products as individual libraries or institutions. We can work together to spend our
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: money wisely. The provincial government has made this possible by working hard to provide
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: all Ontarians with a stable, high-speed communications infrastructure. While there is still a
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: great deal of work needed to improve this infrastructure, libraries are beginning to plan how
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: best to exploit communications technology. We can reduce barriers to access and create the
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: The concept of an ODL is a key element in
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: , a strategic plan to
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: envision and attain Ontario’s economic goal prepared by the Ontario Jobs and Investment
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Board. The OJIB strategic plan recommends an electronic library to “connect students to new
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: workplaces, new sources of information and applied learning opportunities”. The ODL will do
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: that and much more. The ODL will connect students to electronic information, and will also
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: leverage this investment to help connect all citizens to the electronic information they need as
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Libraries have long been a key delivery point for public services. The ODL will allow that
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: delivery point to move closer to citizens, into the smallest of libraries and even into living
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Libraries have a long tradition of using a practical, consultative approach to solving problems,
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Jurisdiction-wide digital libraries are very practical and very real. The ODL we propose is
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: leading edge for Ontario but several jurisdictions have forged the path with collaborative
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: digital libraries. We can learn from their experiences. For example, The Alberta Library (TAL)
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: was incorporated in 1997 as a province-wide library consortium. TAL works collaboratively
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: with its members to promote universal access to materials and resources in more than 250
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: public, post-secondary, special, government and regional libraries. Unlike ODL, TAL does
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: not include elementary or secondary schools. However, the timeline and funding we are
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: proposing for ODL is comparable to TAL‘s experience. TAL’s business planning took several
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: years. TAL’s implementation plan was supported by 8 million dollars in provincially shared
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: funding, spread over three years, later revised to 15 million dollars over 4 years. Not only is
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Alberta’s population 30% of Ontario’s, TAL does not include elementary or secondary
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: schools as partners; it has 250 member libraries. ODL envisions universal access for all
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: 10,000,000 Ontarians, with more than 5000 Ontario libraries as partners. Although ODL can
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: learn from TAL’s practices, the scope of planning and implementing ODL is much larger than
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: The ODL will be a partnership-based organization providing seamless electronic information
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: common resources The ODL will recognize the unique responsibilities and needs of public
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: libraries, school libraries, college and university libraries and special libraries. The ODL
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: envisions a digital network of information resources, allowing members to collaborate when
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will bring consistent, high-quality electronic resources and services to 10 million
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will eliminate barriers to the access to information and create more consistent
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will facilitate consensus among members regarding ODL’s portfolio of electronic
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: licenses and services
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will balance the interests and realities of small and large as well as rich and poor
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will leverage provincial, institutional, and local dollars to realize economies of scale
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: and put Ontario dollars to work for everyone
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will be willing to pool talents and dollars in order to provide common services that are
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: We will provide the licenses, infrastructure, services and support that enable local
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: libraries to provide exceptional services to their clientele. ODL will focus on support,
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to the “deep” web (i.e. Commercial, purchased electronic services)
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to credible web sites and electronic resources
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to digital government information
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to local digital collections
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to support e-learning
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to support citizens, in real-time, as they try to use electronic resources
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: to assist citizens as they use web-links
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: for library workers
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: for the general public
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: of electronic content on a consortia basis for all member libraries
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: of common standards for the preservation and archiving local digital content
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: of a common interface to ODL resources and services that can be imbedded in local
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: library web sites
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: One local point of entry to access seamless electronic library services and
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: resources for their personal, educational and professional needs;
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Access to credible, high-quality, user-friendly electronic services through their
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: community, school, or academic library;
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Confidence that the electronic services and sources they – and their children
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: – are using are safe, valid, and bringing them both global and local
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: perspectives;
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Electronic information and tools that enhance job skills and the learning
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: experience
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: One local point of entry to quality, curriculum- based electronic information
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: resources and services
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Connection to their individual educational environment, whether at the
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: elementary, secondary or post-secondary levels
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading

low_confidence_entries.text: Confidence that the services and resources people are using are credible,
low_confidence_entries.label: non-heading
